title: Come, God Creator, Holy Ghost
words: variously ascribed to Ambrose, Gregory the Great, Charlemagne, or Rhabanus Maurus, 1524
music: ancient Latin found in Erfurt Enchiridion, 1524 Johann Sebastian Bach

lyrics.v1: Come, God Crea- tor, Holy Ghost, And visit Thou these souls of men; Fill them with graces, as Thou dost, Thy creatures make pure again.
lyrics.v2: For Comforter Thy name we call. Sweet gift of God most high above, A holy unction to us all O Fount of life, Fire of love.
lyrics.v3: Our minds illumine and refresh, Deep in our hearts let love burn bright; Thou know'st the weakness of our flesh; And strengthen us with Thy might.
lyrics.v4: Thou with Thy wondrous sev'nfold gifts The finger art of God's right hand; The Father's Word Thou sendest swift On tongues of fire to each land.
lyrics.v5: Drive far from us our wily foe; Grant us Thy blesséd peace within, That in Thy footsteps we may go, And shun the dark ways of sin.

phrases.label: A
phrases.strategy: melody rises — rests on a fermata
